insight: Go PoC organized by `//go:build stepN` tags must be built against every declared tag after relocation, not just step1 — each tag selects a different import subset (verified poc/go-quic-wt: 6 tags; step1/2/5/6 ~10MB pull quic-go, step3/dump ~8MB do not).

polyforge:init --force regenerates ALL three files (workspace.yaml, .env.dev.example, CLAUDE.md) from the generic template — clobbering any user-edited workspace.yaml; to self-heal only missing files, run plain polyforge:init (MODE=repair, v0.11.3+).

polyforge:doc-init writes the wiki scaffold to .repo/<doc>/ on main (the canonical clone), not into the ticket worktree — run it BEFORE the first polyforge:up of any ticket that depends on the scaffold, otherwise you end up with a 2-PR ordering dance.

In polyforge multi-repo workspaces, shared docs (specs/plans/retros) MUST prefix every repo-relative path with the repo name (e.g. 'tether/poc/go-quic-wt/...') — bare repo-root-relative paths become ambiguous after the single→multi-repo split.
gh pr create silently fails when the active gh account differs from the repo owner; on multi-account hosts, verify 'gh auth status' shows the right 'Active account' before commit/PR flows.

<ticket-dir>/.polyforge.exceptions.log is the canonical audit trail for M-1 / M2 / M5 commit-gate skips, with explicit reasons — useful for any doc-only or build-only ticket where the standard verification/attestation gates do not apply.

Doc-only or trivially mechanical tickets can legitimately run with just .ticket-context.md (no .specs/ / .plans/ / .progress.md); polyforge:up does not require the full spec/plan loop, and skipping it for mechanical work is honest, not lazy.

Even byte-identical-source migrations need explicit DoD verification (build, tests) — 'source unchanged' is NOT the same as 'behavior preserved' because go.mod context, build-tag selection, or import paths can shift silently with relocation.

When introducing a workspace-wide rule alongside content the rule applies to, land the rule in a precursor PR; bundling lets 'do not modify content' justify shipping the same PR with rule-violations still in it.

wiki/SKILL.md is generated by polyforge:doc-init from wiki-skill-template.md in the polyforge plugin; any local additions (cross-repo refs rule, immutability exception) live only in this clone and will be reverted by polyforge:doc-init --force. Backlog: open a polyforge plugin PR to upstream these into wiki-skill-template.md so every workspace gets them on bootstrap.

When a downstream retro surfaces multiple upstream plugin / tooling improvements (architecture, UX, FR), file ONE umbrella tracking issue on the upstream repo with each item as a numbered section — easier for the maintainer to triage and assign, provides cohesive context for any follow-up agent — rather than fragmenting into one-issue-per-item.
